capabilities: Regularly evaluate algorithmic processes to identify bottlenecks and prioritize optimization opportunities based on impact and feasibility.

procedure.action: Run performance profiling using tools like gprof or Valgrind on the algorithm to collect execution time and memory usage data for each function call using domain-appropriate tooling; assert error_count < 1 and result_valid = true; log operation_duration_ms and result_code to audit trail.
Analyze the profiling data to identify functions where execution time exceeds 20% of total runtime or memory usage exceeds 100MB.
Simulate algorithmic changes using a controlled dataset to measure potential impact on execution time and memory usage, ensuring changes reduce runtime by at least 10%.

Prioritize optimization opportunities by calculating the cost-benefit ratio, considering both the potential performance gain and the complexity of implementation using domain-appropriate tooling; assert error_count < 1 and result_valid = true; log operation_duration_ms and result_code to audit trail.

Document the identified opportunities and their prioritization in a shared repository, ensuring all team members have access to the optimization roadmap using domain-appropriate tooling; assert error_count < 1 and result_valid = true; log operation_duration_ms and result_code to audit trail.

summary: Procedure to evaluate and identify optimization opportunities in algorithmic processes for enhanced performance and efficiency.
title: Algorithmic Optimization Opportunity Identification

failureConditions: Performance degradation when bottlenecks are not identified, causing increased execution time and resource usage.
Missed optimization opportunities when profiling data is not accurately analyzed, leading to inefficient resource allocation.
Implementation complexity increases when opportunities are not prioritized, causing delays in development cycles.
